applied.notes: Second round scheduled. Panel includes VP Design and Head of Growth PM. Strong culture signal from recruiter.
Intro call with hiring manager next week. Small team, high craft bar. Fully remote.
Applied via Greenhouse. Developer-tools design — strong portfolio angle.

prospect.notes: Remote US. Design tools expertise is a direct match. Team of 12 reports to CPO.
prospect.scoreRationale: Title, compensation, and domain are all strong matches — remote and senior scope align with stated preferences.

prospect.notes: Remote. Core strength match: design systems at scale, cross-functional governance, component library leadership.
prospect.scoreRationale: Design systems leadership is a primary strength and the scope matches director-level expectations.

prospect.notes: AI-native product context — great portfolio story. IC role, not director, but comp clears the bar.
prospect.scoreRationale: Compensation and domain are compelling; seniority is IC rather than director, which is a modest step down from preferred scope.

prospect.notes: Stretch role — broader org scope than current experience. Enterprise B2B is a strength.
prospect.scoreRationale: Comp and title are excellent; VP scope may be a stretch but worth pursuing given the enterprise B2B alignment.

prospect.notes: Manager title and comp both below target. Could negotiate up; would need to clarify team size and growth path.
prospect.scoreRationale: Title and compensation fall below minimum thresholds — worth a conversation only if scope or equity compensates.

local.notes: Austin HQ, hybrid 2 days/week. Design org of 40+. Strong match on DesignOps and team scaling experience.
local.scoreRationale: Local hybrid within commute range, title and comp both strong — DesignOps scope is a direct match.

local.notes: Round Rock — 20 miles. Enterprise hardware/software UX. Large design org, good growth runway.
local.scoreRationale: Within hybrid range and comp clears the bar; enterprise B2B context is a match though hardware domain is less direct.

local.notes: Austin. Consumer product — lower comp floor, less enterprise alignment.
local.scoreRationale: Within commute range but comp is below target and consumer product context is a weaker fit than enterprise B2B.

staffing.notes: 6-month CTH with Fortune 500 financial services client. Remote. Strong comp and potential conversion.
Permanent consulting role. Austin office. Healthcare and financial services clients align well.
3-month engagement. Hourly slightly below target. Legacy enterprise tooling — not a strong growth role.

passed.notes: Comp below minimum for IC at this seniority. Passed.
Declined to pursue — AR hardware context is too far from core enterprise/DesignOps strengths.
